---
title: "Reference"
subtitle: "Quick lookup for skills, layers, and the Codex migration map"
---

# Quick Lookup

## Core workflow skills

| Upstream intent | Codex-facing entry point |
|---|---|
| new project pipeline | `$clo-new-project` |
| discovery | `$clo-discover` |
| identification strategy | `$clo-strategize` |
| analysis and outputs | `$clo-analyze` |
| drafting | `$clo-write` |
| review and peer review | `$clo-review` |
| revision cycle | `$clo-revise` |
| talks | `$clo-talk` |
| submission package | `$clo-submit` |
| maintenance and upgrade | `$clo-research-tools` |

## Install locations

| Item | Generic location |
|---|---|
| public starter bundle | `.codex/` in this repo |
| user skills | `~/.codex/skills` |
| custom subagents | `~/.codex/agents` |
| repo-local skills | `.agents/skills/` |
| session constitution | `AGENTS.md` |
| repo overlay | `AGENTS.override.md` |

# Codex Migration Cheatsheet

This is the table that makes the upstream Clo-Author manual usable without constant mental translation.

| If Clo-Author says | In Dittonomics use | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `CLAUDE.md` | `AGENTS.md` plus `AGENTS.override.md` | Keep the constitution lean and move detail outward |
| `.claude/rules` | active workflow references plus repo overlays | Use source mirrors only when provenance matters |
| `.claude/references` | active `clo-workflow` references | Domain, journal, exploration, and migration guidance |
| slash commands | explicit `$clo-*` skills | Preserve names, change invocation style |
| `.claude/agents` | `~/.codex/agents` custom subagents | Main Codex session remains orchestrator |
| distributable `.claude/` bundle in the repo | forkable `.codex/` starter folder in the repo | Public starter kit for AGENTS, skills, agents, and quick reference |
| `.claude/settings.json` hooks | explicit utilities and config guidance | Avoid undocumented hidden behavior |
| `.claude` exploration instructions | `explorations/` folder plus exploration reference | Create the folder when missing |
| orchestrator agent | main Codex session | No separate orchestrator install needed |

# Worker-Critic Map

| Worker | Reviewer |
|---|---|
| `librarian` | `librarian_critic` |
| `explorer` | `explorer_critic` |
| `strategist` | `strategist_critic` |
| `coder` | `coder_critic` |
| `writer` | `writer_critic` |
| `storyteller` | `storyteller_critic` |

# Journal Calibration Notes

The public Codex adaptation should stay generic first. Any user can adapt the journal layer to their own field, target journals, and review culture.

One example overlay may include:

- `Journal of Development Economics`
- `Regional Studies`
- `Journal of Economic Geography`

Those examples are illustrative only. They are there to show how a concrete user profile can customize the domain and journal reference layer, not to define the public default.

# Exploration Workflow Mapping

| Workflow need | Dittonomics rule |
|---|---|
| prototype estimation idea | create or use `explorations/[name]/` |
| temporary side output | keep it inside the exploration until promoted |
| abandoned branch | archive under `explorations/ARCHIVE/` |
| no exploration folder yet | create `explorations/README.md` and `explorations/ARCHIVE/` first |

# Upgrade Workflow Mapping

When upstream Clo-Author evolves, the Codex adaptation should:

1. refresh the upstream source snapshot
2. regenerate active Codex skills and subagents
3. preserve source mirrors
4. keep user-level defaults separate from repo overlays
5. verify active docs no longer carry stale Claude-only instructions

# Attribution and Scope

This reference is intentionally compact. It is here to make the upstream Clo-Author manual readable to a Codex user, not to replace deeper architecture or customization material.
